title: Lineage-Based Self-Check and Rotating Verifier Selection

story.as_a: tenant operator trusting loopctl's chain of custody to enforce that implementers and verifiers are genuinely independent
story.i_want_to: have the self-check compare dispatch lineage paths (not bare agent_ids) and have loopctl choose which verifier lineage handles each story, not the orchestrator
story.so_that: a creative orchestrator cannot dispatch a sock-puppet sub-agent and pre-select it as its own verifier, and the lineage model actually prevents self-approval instead of just theater

rationale: Dispatch lineage (US-26.2.1) exists but the self-check still compares agent_ids. Two dispatches from the same orchestrator have different agent_ids but share a common ancestor — they're effectively the same actor. This story teaches `validate_not_self_*` to compare lineage paths and picks the verifier from an eligible pool, removing the orchestrator's ability to pre-choose. Without this change, the ephemeral-key work from US-26.2.1 is pointless — the old agent_id comparison would still pass for sock-puppet sub-agents.

acceptance_criteria.description: Helper `Loopctl.Dispatches.lineage_shares_prefix?/2` takes two lineage paths (arrays of uuids) and returns true if they share any common prefix of length ≥ 1. Example: `["root", "orch", "impl"]` and `["root", "orch", "rev"]` share prefix → true. `["root_a", "impl"]` and `["root_b", "rev"]` share nothing → false.

`Loopctl.Progress.validate_not_self_report/2`, `validate_not_self_review/2`, and `validate_not_self_verify/2` accept a caller dispatch lineage and the story's implementer dispatch lineage, comparing via `lineage_shares_prefix?`. Shared prefix → `{:error, :self_*_blocked}`. No prefix match → `:ok`.
The implementer's lineage is recorded on the story at claim time: `stories.implementer_dispatch_id` (FK to dispatches). This is set in `claim_story` and is immutable afterwards (trigger enforces).

`Loopctl.Progress.request_review/2` selects a verifier lineage by querying active non-expired, non-revoked dispatches in the tenant that: (a) have `role = :orchestrator` OR `role = :agent` with a verifier-capable tag, and (b) do NOT share a prefix with the implementer's lineage. If multiple candidates exist, select one using a cryptographically-seeded RNG with the tenant's audit-signing key + story_id as seed (deterministic but unpredictable to the orchestrator).

If no eligible verifier exists at `request_review` time, the story remains in `reported_done` with a new status flag `verifier_needed: true`. An operator alert is triggered (webhook from existing Epic 10/17 infrastructure) pointing at a system article `verifier-needed`. The orchestrator can request additional dispatches to unblock this.
The selected verifier is recorded on the story as `stories.verifier_dispatch_id`, immutable after set. Chain-of-custody checks compare against this stored id rather than recomputing.

Verifier selection is logged in the audit chain with action `verifier_selected`, payload including the pool size, the selected dispatch id, and a commitment hash of the RNG seed (so the operator can later audit that the selection was deterministic and unbiased).

Integration test: a verifier with a lineage descending from the same orchestrator as the implementer gets `{:error, :self_verify_blocked}` on verify attempt. A verifier with a completely separate root ancestor succeeds.

Edge case: when the first dispatch after tenant signup tries to claim and report a story before any other lineage exists, the story stays in `reported_done` with `verifier_needed: true` until another lineage is registered. System article explains the expected workflow: the operator dispatches a second independent lineage (typically via a second CLI session or a second machine) and the system auto-picks it.

Migration adds `stories.implementer_dispatch_id`, `stories.verifier_dispatch_id`, `stories.verifier_needed` columns. The Phase 2 cutover is an atomic operation: Phase 1 ships the enforcement flag for `lineage_prefix_overlap` in :shadow mode; the operator runs a one-shot backfill script that creates dispatches for any in-flight stories (associating them with the legacy long-lived key as an implicit root dispatch); then the flag flips to :strict. After the flip, all stories have non-null dispatch references and the legacy agent_id comparison is removed from the call site entirely. No dual path remains.

OpenAPI spec updates: extended Story schema with dispatch fields. New error codes `self_verify_blocked` (via lineage), `verifier_needed`. CHANGELOG entry. System article stub `verifier-needed` reserved for US-26.3.3.

test_cases.name: Verifier from sibling dispatch of implementer is blocked
preconditions: sibling (pseudo-reviewer) dispatch from the same orchestrator
steps: Attempt verify_story with the sibling dispatch
expected_results: Audit chain has no verify entry

test_cases.name: Verifier from a separate root ancestor is allowed
preconditions: Two separate root dispatches (operator opened two independent CLI sessions)

test_cases.name: Rotating selection: verifier_dispatch_id is set by loopctl, not the caller
expected_results: stories.verifier_dispatch_id is set to one of the eligible dispatches
The selection is deterministic given the RNG seed
A different caller cannot override the selection

test_cases.name: verifier_needed flag is set when no eligible lineage exists
preconditions: Only the implementer's lineage exists in the tenant
expected_results: Alert webhook is fired (check webhook queue)

technical_notes: Module: lib/loopctl/dispatches.ex for lineage_shares_prefix/2 helper. Keep it pure (no DB access).
Update validate_not_self_* signatures: they now take (story, caller_lineage) where caller_lineage is resolved from the authenticated dispatch in the controller.
The caller's lineage is resolved at controller time: look up the dispatch associated with the authenticated api_key, extract lineage_path. Pass to Progress functions.

Rotating verifier selection: use `:crypto.hash/2` seeded with `tenant_audit_private_key || story_id || current_day` to derive a deterministic but unpredictable index into the eligible pool. Document that this reseeds daily to prevent long-term prediction.
Deterministic but unpredictable: the selection is reproducible given the seed, but the orchestrator cannot predict it without the private audit key.
Webhook alert for verifier_needed: use the existing Loopctl.Webhooks infrastructure with event_type `story.verifier_needed`.
Legacy stories (implementer_dispatch_id is nil) during the deprecation window: fall back to the old agent_id comparison. After the cutover, require the dispatch-based check.
